stores.title: Noctua Nh-d15 6 heatpipe with Dual Nf-a15 140mm fans, Size: 140 mm

about_the_product.description: The CPU cooler from Noctua no wonder were able to convince several times in various test reports with their very good cooling performance, so that the manufacturer Noctua NH-D15 with the new strikes proven ways. The processing of the new model follows on seamlessly from the excellent quality of its predecessor. The connections between heat pipes and fins are soldered and the plates at the edges additionally braced. Due to its wider heatpipe layout and two NF-A15 140-mm-Premium fans with PWM support for fully automatic speed control the NH-D15 can improve the efficiency of the award-winning NH-D14 again. With these tricks Noctua achieves a very good stability and durability.

items.snippet: there are only 2 downsides to this cooler.. the ugly fans, and the massive size. just about managed to fit into a NZXT H440 with less than 10mm of clearance to
If you dont mind the look there is little which is more effective than this cooler. Excellent build quality as always
other than that everything else is as you would expect from noctua. insultingly easy to install, quiet and cool. Scan service and delivery great as always
Make it sure it fits with your RAM as it may be too big so you only can use one fan. Comes well packaged and super easy to set up
One of the best coolers I've had. It is barely audible and it cools really well. Easy to assemble and has a clear manual.

items.title: Noctua's NH-D15 Versus Five High End Closed Loop Liquid Coolers

user_reviews.title: 9850X3D All good
user_reviews.text:
I bought this for a friends new setup on a whim, based on previous experience of it on another socket many years ago, and knowledge its a bit of a mainstay. I was however not aware that the NH-D15 G2 exists and is recommended as the goto iteration for AM5 before purchasing! eeek

However, I needn't of worried. Its reported that the G2 iteration is slightly better with 2 to 3 degrees gains -- but its also a good £30 to £40 more expensive. Not a lot more money, so you may want to consider it. But in practice in a scenario where stock clocks is the goal, this NH-D15 non-G2 is performing decent at 47 degrees idle on a 9850X3D (generally hotter CPU since higher binned and so higher clocks) with only the central fan connected (we forgot second fan and will come back to it).

So yes, after all these years its still a decent cooler and has that Noctua quality to it. Very clear instructions and well thought out packaging as well. There's probably cheaper alternatives but -- this like most noctua coolers has a generous 6 year warranty and a very good customer service reputation.

user_reviews.title: Excellent for its physics, but not so much for assembly method.
user_reviews.text:
Cannot be failed by its ability to chill and cool, but when it comes to fitting it on motherboard...ohh boy.
It's size means that you will be operating in very tight places. Attachment to a CPU is easy enough, but the gimmicky manipulation starts, when you try attaching the fan to the grill with this ridicules ancient-boomer clip, that not only defy your manual skills, but also damages the grills themselves.
And you cannot pre-attached the fan to the grill, because otherwise you wont be able to screw it to a CPU, as it blocks access to screws.
Stupidity.
We are at 2024 and yet the companies insists on using these rubbish pre-modern age solutions.
The clip would be acceptable on smaller cooler, but not on this size.
There are better attachment solutions available, you know? Like screws.
A solution that Cooler Master have chosen.
I am taking 2 stars off for that.
I had to reattach it several times, my fingers are scratched and my logical mind screams: WHY!
For my second backup built, I chose Cooler Master. Because of that.

user_reviews.title: Best Cooler I have ever had
user_reviews.text: Better cooling performance than the all in one that I had previously and absolutely silent during normal PC use.

Truely magnificent! Product was wrapped nicely with 2 layers of bubble wrap with fragile labelling. I have yet to mount it but all components are present with no damage. Can't wait to see how it performs.

user_reviews.title: Famous for a reason
user_reviews.text: Very effective cooler. Large though, if you have a small Mobo or case you may struggle.

user_reviews.title: Beats similary priced AIO water coolers
user_reviews.text:
Fitting:

The bracket and tools supplied fitted easily, allowing the heatsink (which is sizable) to be easily bolted down at good pressure. Fans (two included) slide down and fit on the front of each set of fins, with a set of wire clips. ( a little fiddly but I have used worse).

The new design has a cut out so cleared my ram modules without any issue.

Performance:

Much better than the Coolmaster Liquid Pro 140 AIO (twin 14"fan) it replaced. Keeps my OC Intel I7 8700k (5.0GHz) around 65c on full load, and just a few degrees above ambient on idle.

Definitely closer to any Custom loop setup I have had than any AIO I have tried.

Noise:

No louder than any of the AIO (CM/Corsair) units I have used with twin fans, if anything because of the large fans, I hear it spin up the fans less.

Size:

BIG check the size before you buy, that being said, it fitted into my Corsair 400C and because of the redesign slotted over my RAM.

Overall:

Better then similar priced AIO coolers, with easy fitting and amazingly good build quality, not sure why I even bothered with AIO cooler to start with.

If you have the space you will not get a better air cooler ( or from the ones I have had an AIO either)

user_reviews.title: Noctua NH-D15 Dual Radioator Quiet CPU Cooler
user_reviews.text: I was concerned that I was not giving the Ryzen 7 1700-X the proper cooling it deserved having been sent the CPU from a different supplier to scan that on the web page said it came with fan when it didn't. I had been using an Asus Red LIon Square cooler prior to the Noctua install. I wasn't able to use the second fan due to the height of the RAM modules but it was an awful lot easier to install than the standard 'rocker-lock' that you normally expect with AMD cooling hardware! There was a delay in delivery due to waiting for the AMD mount points but I was prepared to wait. I was originally looking at what was available for the CPU and was looking at CoolerMaster. I was advised by the pre-sales Team that there was a better cooler, the Noctua but a bit more expensive. It is well worth the extra cost for piece of mind - I also purchased Noctua CPU cleaning wipes to remove the old thermal paste. The instructions for fitting are brilliantly clear and the hardware comes well packaged and protected from damage. I would highly recommend this fan to anyone thinking of upgrading their CPU cooler. I've never been keen on Liquid cooled CPU's after hearing horror stories. Air Cooled is bes and I can see no point in having a cooler with rainbow colours - You spend your time looking at the screen of a computer, not its innards - unless you are intending to have A CPU/PSU/Mobo rainbow disco party!
